results.name: panic_assertion_failed
results.rationale: 3 scalar args (rdi, rsi, rdx, no derefs) matches a &'static str pair plus a &'static Location pointer; function builds a fmt::Arguments from the str and tail-calls a panic_fmt helper through a vtable slot, classic Rust panic helper shape.

results.name: panic_index_out_of_bounds
results.submitted_decl:
use core::panic::Location;

(index: usize, len: usize, location: &'static Location<'static>) -> !
results.rationale: Three scalar args (rdi=index, rsi=len, rdx=&Location forwarded into panic_fmt's 2nd param); diverges to panic_fmt — return is `!`.

results.name: panic_unwrap_err
results.rationale: Function builds a fmt::Arguments with 2 ArgumentV1 entries (each pointing to one of the two on-stack &str pairs from rdi/rsi and rdx/rcx) and tail-calls core::panicking::panic_fmt(&args, loc=r8); r8 is a single pointer to a static Location.

results.name: panic_overflow_sub
results.rationale: Function panics with literal "attempt to subtract with overflow" via core::panicking::panic_fmt — this is core::num::overflow_panic::sub, which takes no explicit args (rdi = implicit &Location from #[track_caller]).

results.name: option_unit_qty_add_a
results.submitted_decl:
pub struct OptionUnitQty { tag: u64, unit: u64, qty: u64 }

(this: &OptionUnitQty, other: &OptionUnitQty) -> (u64, u64)
results.submitted_types: pub struct OptionUnitQty { tag: u64, unit: u64, qty: u64 }
results.rationale: Two &OptionUnitQty (24B, three u64 fields each at +0/+8/+0x10) read fully via rdi/rsi; returns 16-byte pair via rax/rdx as caller demarshals two u64s from the result.

results.name: compute_margin_requirement_by_mode
results.submitted_decl:
#[repr(C)]
pub struct OptionUnitQty {
    pub tag: i64,
    pub unit: u64,
    pub value: i64,
}

#[repr(C)]
pub struct OraclePrice {
    pub qty: OptionUnitQty,
    pub price: i64,
}

#[repr(C)]
pub struct Position {
    pub notional: OptionUnitQty,
    pub legs: [OptionUnitQty; 4],
    pub margin: OptionUnitQty,
    pub thresholds: u64,
    pub max_leverage: u8,
}

#[repr(C)]
pub struct MarginResult {
    pub sum: OptionUnitQty,
    pub sum_pos: OptionUnitQty,
    pub notional: OptionUnitQty,
    pub margin: OptionUnitQty,
    pub extra: OptionUnitQty,
}

(position: &Position, oracle: &OraclePrice, mode: u32) -> MarginResult
results.submitted_types:
#[repr(C)]
pub struct OptionUnitQty {
    pub tag: i64,
    pub unit: u64,
    pub value: i64,
}

#[repr(C)]
pub struct OraclePrice {
    pub qty: OptionUnitQty,
    pub price: i64,
}

#[repr(C)]
pub struct Position {
    pub notional: OptionUnitQty,
    pub legs: [OptionUnitQty; 4],
    pub margin: OptionUnitQty,
    pub thresholds: u64,
    pub max_leverage: u8,
}

#[repr(C)]
pub struct MarginResult {
    pub sum: OptionUnitQty,
    pub sum_pos: OptionUnitQty,
    pub notional: OptionUnitQty,
    pub margin: OptionUnitQty,
    pub extra: OptionUnitQty,
}
results.rationale: 5xOptionUnitQty result (120B); rsi=&Position with OptionUnitQty notional/margin and threshold+leverage tail; rdx=&OraclePrice (qty+price); rcx=mode enum (None/CustomTable/Liquidation/ADL).

results.name: compute_user_margin_and_shortfall
results.rationale: sret 96B = 4x OptionUnitQty (tag/unit/value triples) for equity/margin_req/abs_notional/raw_equity; rsi=&UserState w/ 2 OptionUnitQty fields + Vec<AssetPosition>; rdx=&OracleData; 4th bool arg observed at call sites (nullptr/1) but optimized away binary-side.

results.name: compute_account_value_for_direction
results.submitted_signature: (user_state: &UserState, is_isolated: bool, asset_idx: u64, margin_mode: u8, oracle: &Oracle) -> UnitQty
results.rationale: 5 reg args (rdx/r8 optimized away but caller passes them per ARITY-TRAP); returns 24B UnitQty {tag,unit,value} via sret; rsi=&UserState w/ positions:BTreeMap at +0x30; r9=&Oracle w/ Box<[OracleEntry]> at +8.
